name: Hamilton (Micro-Framework for Dataflows)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); DAGWorks; Hamilton UI commercial

auth.notes: Local framework; DAGWorks Hub for managed lineage

performance.notes: Very lightweight; DAG execution overhead minimal

agent_readiness.notes: Python functions as DAG nodes — function name = node name, type-annotated inputs resolved automatically. def revenue(price, quantity): return price * quantity. dr = Driver({}, module). dr.execute(['revenue'], inputs={'price': 10, 'quantity': 5}). Auto-builds DAG from function signatures. Works with pandas, Dask, Ray, Spark. Decorators: @config.when for conditional logic, @extract_columns for dataframe column splitting. Feature engineering, ML preprocessing, data transformation pipelines. Lightweight — no server needed.

gotchas: Function name determines DAG node name — naming discipline critical
Type annotation required for proper DAG construction
Less well-known than Kedro, Prefect, or Dagster
best_when: Feature engineering pipelines with clear function dependencies
When you want DAG structure with just plain Python functions
ML preprocessing as composable Python functions
Teams wanting Kedro-like DAGs without Kedro's boilerplate
avoid_when: Complex workflow orchestration (Prefect/Airflow handles retries, scheduling)
When Kedro's project template and conventions are valuable
summary: Hamilton builds DAGs from Python function signatures — function name = node name, type annotations = edges. def revenue(price, quantity): return price * quantity — automatically connected in DAG. Driver({'module'}).execute(['revenue'], inputs={...}). Works with pandas/Dask/Ray. Lightweight: no server, no config files. Best for feature engineering and data transformation as composable functions.
